steps.q: Is there fresh water in the tank?
steps.no: Fill the fresh-water tank, then retest.

steps.q: Does the water pump run when a tap opens?
steps.no: Check the pump switch, VB06 pump fuse, battery voltage and pump connections.

steps.q: Is the frost-protection drain closed and is water staying in the boiler?
steps.no: Warm and reset the drain valve, then refill and purge the system.

steps.q: Is the heater or boiler receiving 12 V and gas?
steps.yes: Allow the boiler to heat, then inspect controls and fault indications.
steps.no: Check the VB04 heating fuse, gas cylinder, regulator and appliance isolation valve.

steps.q: Is the leisure battery above 12 V?
steps.no: Charge and test the leisure battery.

steps.q: Are the main battery terminals and main fuse secure?
steps.no: Correct the loose or failed connection before proceeding.

steps.q: Is power present at the Calira EVS output?
steps.no: Check the EVS supply, charger and main connections.

steps.q: Is power present at the VB06 input?
steps.yes: Check the individual output fuse and appliance earth.
steps.no: Trace the cable and connector between EVS and VB06.

title: Refrigerator not working on 12 V while driving

steps.q: Is the engine running and alternator voltage above resting battery voltage?
steps.no: Investigate the vehicle charging system first.

steps.q: Is the VB04 D+ fuse intact?
steps.no: Replace only with the specified 5 A fuse and investigate if it fails again.

steps.q: Is the VB06 refrigerator fuse intact?
steps.yes: Test D+ at the refrigerator relay and the 12 V heating element circuit.
steps.no: Replace with the specified fuse and investigate the load if it fails again.
